intro:
Around 180 stations sit within a 30-minute commute of Minatomirai, expanding to 477 stations within 45 minutes. Average rent across the 45-minute commute zone is roughly ¥98,200. With the Minatomirai Line running through service onto the Tokyu Toyoko Line, the area connects to Yokohama Station, Musashi-kosugi, and Shibuya, forming a distinctive commute zone as eastern Kanagawa's waterfront business district.

Minatomirai is a master-planned business and commercial district born of Yokohama's waterfront redevelopment. It hosts the headquarters and R&D bases of major manufacturers — led by the Nissan Motor headquarters and the Fuji Xerox (now FUJIFILM Business Innovation) research center — along with IT services, foreign consulting firms, financial back-office operations, and hotel and MICE-related companies. Commuters skew heavily toward engineers, R&D staff, corporate functions, and hospitality professionals.

The commute zone is anchored by the Minatomirai Line's through service with the Tokyu Toyoko Line, reaching Kikuna, Musashi-kosugi, Jiyugaoka, Nakameguro, and Shibuya without transfers. Transferring at Yokohama Station opens up broad access: the JR Tokaido and Yokosuka Lines toward Fujisawa and Chigasaki, the Keikyu Main Line toward Kamiooka and Kanazawa-bunko, the Sotetsu Main Line toward Futamatagawa and Ebina, and the JR Yokohama Line toward Shin-yokohama and Machida.

If you want to keep rent down, realistic options include Myorenji, Hakuraku, Higashi-hakuraku, and Tammachi on the Tokyu Toyoko Line; Tennocho, Hoshikawa, Kami-hoshikawa, and Futamatagawa on the Sotetsu Main Line (transfer at Yokohama); Minami-ota and Kamiooka on the Keikyu Main Line (transfer at Yokohama); and the Negishi, Isogo, and Shin-sugita area on the JR Negishi Line. For those who prioritize living close to work, the Bashamichi, Nihon-odori, and Kannai areas are also popular choices.

| Area | Rent guide (single) | To Minatomirai | Main lines |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Myorenji | ¥60,000–80,000 range | approx. 10–15 min | Tokyu Toyoko Line / Minatomirai Line through service |
| Musashi-kosugi | ¥80,000–100,000 range | approx. 20–25 min | Tokyu Toyoko Line / Minatomirai Line through service |
| Futamatagawa | ¥60,000–80,000 range | approx. 20–25 min | Sotetsu Main Line (transfer at Yokohama) |
| Kamiooka | ¥60,000–80,000 range | approx. 20–25 min | Keikyu Main Line (transfer at Yokohama) |
| Shin-sugita | ¥60,000–80,000 range | approx. 25–30 min | JR Negishi Line |

With Kayoha, set Minatomirai as your commute destination to compare 1831 stations on a color-coded commute-time map in 5-minute steps, and narrow down candidate areas with an AI recommendation of 20 stations at once, alongside rent guides and neighborhood profiles.

faq.q: Which areas within a 30-minute commute of Minatomirai offer relatively affordable rent?
faq.a: Good benchmarks include Myorenji, Hakuraku, Higashi-hakuraku, and Tammachi on the Tokyu Toyoko Line; Tennocho, Hoshikawa, and Futamatagawa on the Sotetsu Main Line (transfer at Yokohama); Minami-ota and Kamiooka on the Keikyu Main Line (transfer at Yokohama); and Negishi, Isogo, and Shin-sugita on the JR Negishi Line. Single-occupancy rents mostly fall in the ¥60,000–80,000 range, with plenty of options along Kanagawa's rail corridors.

faq.q: Which lines offer the shortest commute to Minatomirai?
faq.a: The mainstays of the 10–25 minute zone are the Minatomirai Line (toward Bashamichi and Nihon-odori), the Tokyu Toyoko Line through service (toward Myorenji and Kikuna), and the JR, Keikyu, and Sotetsu lines via a transfer at Yokohama Station. Thanks to the through service with the Tokyu Toyoko Line, you can also commute from the Shibuya direction without transferring — a major advantage.

faq.q: What is the Minatomirai area like? Is it a good place to live?
faq.a: The area around Minatomirai Station is a master-planned district where office towers, large shopping complexes, and high-rise apartments coexist. With shopping facilities, parks, and a waterfront promenade all in place, daily life can be completed on foot, though rent levels are on the high side. It tends to attract dual-income couples and higher-earning singles.

faq.q: Which areas are popular with families commuting to Minatomirai?
faq.a: Areas such as Kikuna, Okurayama, and Musashi-kosugi on the Tokyu Toyoko Line; Izumino and Shonandai on the Sotetsu Main Line; Nagatsuta and Machida on the JR Yokohama Line (transfer at Yokohama); and the Center-kita and Center-minami direction on the Yokohama Municipal Subway Blue Line (transfer at Yokohama) tend to be well served by nurseries, parks, and supermarkets, making them popular with families.

faq.q: Are there stations with first-departure trains for commuting to Minatomirai?
faq.a: Trains originate from Motomachi-Chukagai, the Minatomirai Line's terminus, giving you the option of commuting seated. On the Tokyu Toyoko Line, choosing services that terminate at Kikuna or originate at Motomachi-Chukagai, and on the JR Negishi Line, choosing trains originating from the Ofuna and Isogo directions, can improve your chances of getting a seat.
